puzzle.title: The Tormented Soul of the Nile
puzzle.intro: In an age of great oppression, when a tyrant ruled the land with absolute cruelty, one soul chose the path of faith over the comfort of royalty. This figure dwelled in the heart of power yet found truth in the message of a humble prophet. Their journey would become a timeless lesson in patience, sacrifice, and unwavering conviction. Can you identify this remarkable individual who preferred divine pleasure over worldly status?
clues: I lived in a palace of great power, yet my heart found truth in a humble message.
I chose to follow the guidance of Musa, knowing the consequences I would face.
I was married to the most powerful tyrant of my time, yet I rejected his false gods.
My suffering was severe, as I was punished for my belief in the One God.
I never wavered in my faith, even when faced with the harshest torment.
My Lord was pleased with my patience and chose me for an honored place in Paradise.

trial.label: Trial/Event
options: Fighting in Allah's cause with an army
Building places of worship in Egypt
Traveling across the sea with Musa

location.label: Key Element
options: The banks of the Nile River

outcome.label: Outcome
options: Became a prophet after many years
Led the Israelites out of Egypt
Was martyred and honored by Allah
Lived to old age in comfort
outcome.answer: Was martyred and honored by Allah

puzzle.arabic: وَضَرَبَ ٱللَّهُ مَثَلًۭا لِّلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱمْرَأَتَ فِرْعَوْنَ إِذْ قَالَتْ رَبِّ ٱبْنِ لِى عِندَكَ بَيْتًۭا فِى ٱلْجَنَّةِ وَنَجِّنِى مِن فِرْعَوْنَ وَعَمَلِهِۦ وَنَجِّنِى مِنَ ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ
puzzle.verse: And Allāh presents an example of those who believed: the wife of Pharaoh, when she said, "My Lord, build for me near You a house in Paradise and save me from Pharaoh and his deeds and save me from the wrongdoing people." (66:11)
